title: A Busy Day in Numbers
description: Read a passage about time and daily routine.

blocks.passage:
📖 A Busy Day with Numbers

आज सोमवार है। (Aaj somvār hai.) I wake up at seven o'clock, eat breakfast, and go to class. My teacher asks, अब कितने बजे हैं? and we answer in Hindi. We practice counting: एक, दो, तीन, चार, पाँच. Then we practice questions: कहाँ, कब, और क्यों. The classroom is full of small tasks, but each task helps me become faster.

At the end of the day, I can say the time, ask what day it is, and count simple objects around me.

questions.q: What day is it in the passage?
Name one question word from the passage.
What can the learner do at the end?
questions.a: Say the time and ask about the day.

items.statement: The learner practices one word at a time.
items.explanation: The passage describes slow, step-by-step learning.

items.statement: Hindi is only used in formal books.
items.explanation: Hindi is used in daily conversation too.

items.statement: The learner learns useful everyday language.
items.explanation: The passage focuses on practical words and sentences.

blocks.title: Cultural Note
blocks.content: In Hindi, people commonly ask about time and plans in short, natural phrases. Practicing numbers with daily routines makes them easier to remember.
